seo.title: 100 Monitoring & Observability resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Monitoring & Observability resources for engineers.

content.intro: Effective monitoring in modern infrastructure requires moving beyond simple uptime checks to a unified observability strategy that handles high-cardinality data, distributed tracing for AI microservices, and precise LLM cost tracking. This guide provides a curated selection of tools and patterns to build a production-ready stack using OpenTelemetry, specialized LLM proxies, and open-source metric engines.

items.title: Sentry Error Tracking
items.description: Implement the Sentry SDK to capture unhandled exceptions and performance bottlenecks. Use the 'breadcrumbs' feature to reconstruct the state leading to a crash.

items.title: Prometheus Time-Series Database
items.description: Deploy Prometheus for scraping metrics from exporters. Use it to store system-level data like CPU/RAM and custom application business metrics.

items.title: Grafana Visualization
items.description: Connect Prometheus and Loki data sources to build real-time dashboards. Use community templates for Node Exporter and PostgreSQL monitoring.

items.title: Better Stack Uptime
items.description: Configure external heartbeat monitoring and status pages. Integrate with Slack to receive immediate notification if the public endpoint fails.

items.title: VictoriaMetrics
items.description: A high-performance, cost-effective drop-in replacement for Prometheus. Use it for long-term storage of metrics with better compression ratios.

items.title: Vector.dev Log Routing
items.description: Deploy Vector as a sidecar or aggregator to collect, transform, and route logs from various sources to Loki, S3, or Datadog.

items.title: Grafana Loki
items.description: A horizontally scalable, highly available, multi-tenant log aggregation system inspired by Prometheus. It indexes metadata rather than the full log content.

items.title: Checkly Synthetic Monitoring
items.description: Write Playwright scripts to simulate user flows in production. Checkly runs these on a schedule to ensure critical paths (like checkout) work.

items.title: Netdata Real-time Monitoring
items.description: Install on individual nodes for per-second granularity monitoring. Ideal for troubleshooting immediate performance spikes on specific servers.

items.title: Highlight.io Session Replay
items.description: Open-source full-stack monitoring that includes session replay, allowing you to see exactly what the user did before an error occurred.

items.title: Helicone Proxy
items.description: Route OpenAI or Anthropic requests through Helicone to get an instant dashboard of costs, latency, and request/response logs.

items.title: LangSmith Tracing
items.description: Use LangChain's observability tool to debug complex chains. It visualizes the inputs and outputs of every step in your LLM pipeline.

items.title: LiteLLM Proxy
items.description: A unified interface to call 100+ LLMs. Use its logging feature to export data to S3, Langfuse, or Mixpanel for centralized analysis.

items.title: Langfuse Open-Source Tracing
items.description: Self-hostable alternative for LLM application tracing. It provides specific metrics for token usage and latency across different model providers.

items.title: Arize Phoenix
items.description: An open-source library for ML observability that helps in evaluating LLM responses and detecting embedding drift in vector databases.

items.title: Portkey AI Gateway
items.description: Control your LLM traffic with features like fallback, retries, and load balancing while collecting detailed observability data.

items.title: Promptfoo Evaluation
items.description: Run test cases against your prompts to measure output quality and regression before deploying new prompt versions to production.

items.title: Weights & Biases (W&B) Prompts
items.description: Visualize the execution flow of your LLM programs and track model performance over time with their specialized prompt logging.

items.title: Lunary LLM Stack
items.description: Open-source observability for LLMs including cost tracking, user analytics, and prompt versioning in a single dashboard.

items.title: Parea AI
items.description: A developer platform to debug, test, and monitor LLM apps. It allows you to run evaluations on production data to find edge case failures.

items.title: OpenTelemetry Collector
items.description: Set up a central collector to receive OTLP data and export it to multiple backends simultaneously (e.g., Jaeger and Datadog).

items.title: Jaeger Distributed Tracing
items.description: Deploy Jaeger to visualize the path of a request across microservices. Essential for finding latency bottlenecks in complex AI pipelines.

items.title: SigNoz Observability Suite
items.description: An open-source alternative to Datadog that combines metrics, traces, and logs in one UI, built on top of OpenTelemetry and ClickHouse.

items.title: Honeycomb.io High-Cardinality Analysis
items.description: Use Honeycomb for debugging complex systems where you need to group by user ID, request ID, or other high-cardinality fields.

items.title: Grafana Tempo
items.description: A high-scale distributed tracing backend that is deeply integrated with Grafana and Loki, allowing you to jump from logs to traces.

items.title: Uptrace
items.description: An open-source distributed tracing tool that uses ClickHouse to store data and provides an easy-to-use UI for OTel data analysis.

items.title: HyperDX Unified Platform
items.description: An open-source tool that correlates logs and traces automatically, making it easier to find the root cause of an error in a microservice.

items.title: ClickHouse for Observability
items.description: Utilize ClickHouse as the storage engine for logs and traces due to its superior columnar compression and query performance for analytical data.

items.title: OpenTelemetry SDK Auto-Instrumentation
items.description: Use OTel zero-code instrumentation for Java, Python, or Node.js to start collecting traces without modifying your application code.

items.title: Pyroscope Continuous Profiling
items.description: Integrate Pyroscope to continuously monitor CPU and memory usage at the function level, identifying lines of code causing performance regressions.

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Golden Signals'—latency, traffic, errors, and saturation—over hundreds of low-level metrics to avoid dashboard clutter.
Implement sampling in your tracing strategy (e.g., 10% of successful traces, 100% of errors) to reduce storage costs without losing critical data.
Use Prometheus Recording Rules to pre-calculate complex queries, significantly improving dashboard load times for high-volume data.
Tag LLM requests with 'metadata-user-id' and 'metadata-feature-name' in your proxy to enable granular cost attribution by customer or feature.
Set up 'Inhibition Rules' in Alertmanager to prevent a flood of alerts; for example, if a data center is down, suppress individual service alerts.
